methodsAttempted.notes: Robots.txt allowed the official PDF path; direct HTTP fetch and local text extraction were used after Firecrawl PDF query returned only the table-of-contents hit for the annex.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery identified the university's 2025 internal regulations PDF as the only verified binding source with AI generative use rules; project and event pages were rejected for claim extraction.

artifacts.claimText: Université de Rennes' 2025 internal regulations state that the precautionary principle applies to generative AI tools and prohibit using a non-validated generative AI tool.

artifacts.claimText: For teaching missions, Université de Rennes' AI charter says AI must not substitute for a teacher's act without supervision and verification, and AI tasks affecting student assessment or admission must be human verified in a demonstrable way.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Toutes les tâches permettant l'évaluation des étudiants ou leur admission automatisée par IA doit obligatoirement faire l'objet d'une vérification humaine qui doit le cas échéant être démontrable.

artifacts.title: Université de Rennes AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: The official 2025 internal regulations PDF for Université de Rennes contains Annexe 2, a charter on generative AI use. Agent-reviewed staged claims cover validated-tool use, human verification for AI-supported teaching assessment or admission tasks, and student disclosure when AI use is authorized in assessments.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

Firecrawl was useful for discovery and initial scraping, but its targeted PDF query returned only a table-of-contents hit for the AI charter; direct compliant HTTP fetch and local text extraction were used for the annex evidence.
Official AI project and workshop pages were rejected for policy claims because they describe experimentation or events rather than binding rules.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
